pythonCode: import plotly.graph_objects as go from plotly.subplots import make_subplots import pandas as pd import calendar data['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(data['Date']) data['Month'] = data['Date'].dt.month location = 'Darwin' #Perth city_df = data[data['Location'] == location].copy() monthly_stats = city_df.groupby('Month')[['MinTemp', 'MaxTemp', 'Rainfall']].mean().reset_index() monthly_stats['Month_Name'] = monthly_stats['Month'].apply(lambda x: calendar.month_abbr[x]) fig = make_subplots(specs=[[{"secondary_y": True}]]) fig.add_trace( go.Bar( x=monthly_stats['Month_Name'], y=monthly_stats['Rainfall'], name="Avg Rainfall (mm)", marker_color="rgba(56, 189, 248, 0.6)" ), secondary_y=True, ) fig.add_trace( go.Scatter( x=monthly_stats['Month_Name'], y=monthly_stats['MaxTemp'], name="Max Temp (°C)", mode="lines+markers", line=dict(color="#ef4444", width=3, shape="spline"), marker=dict(size=8) ), secondary_y=False, ) fig.add_trace( go.Scatter( x=monthly_stats['Month_Name'], y=monthly_stats['MinTemp'], name="Min Temp (°C)", mode="lines+markers", line=dict(color="#3b82f6", width=3, shape="spline"), marker=dict(size=8) ), secondary_y=False, ) fig.update_layout( title_text=f"Monthly Averages: Temperature vs. Rainfall at {location} (2007-2017)", hovermode="x unified", # Shows all 3 values when hovering over a month! plot_bgcolor="white", width=800, height=600, legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.15, x=0.5, xanchor="center") ) fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Temperature (°C)", showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgray", range=[0, 35], secondary_y=False) fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Rainfall (mm)", showgrid=False, range=[0, 10], secondary_y=True) print(f"Chart at {location}") fig.show()

title.text: Monthly Averages: Temperature vs. Rainfall at Newcastle (2007-2017)
